title: Add a Join by field transformation

blocks.content: The join by field transformation combines two or more data tables that share a common field. It's similar to a SQL `JOIN` clause. Use this to merge multiple query results into a single table, which is especially useful for converting multiple time series results into a single wide table with a shared time field.
This transformation requires a panel with multiple queries. Open an existing panel in edit mode on your dashboard.
Click **Add transformation** and select **Join by field**.

Under **Mode**, select how you want your queries joined. Use the following table as a guideline:

| Mode | When to use |
| --- | --- |
| INNER | Combine results from multiple queries into one result, dropping rows where a successful join cannot occur. |
| OUTER (TIME SERIES) | Include all data from an inner join plus rows where values don't match in every input. |
| OUTER (TABULAR) | Join tables so the result includes matched and unmatched rows from either or both tables. |
Select a **Mode**, then choose the **Field** to join on from the multiple tables.
Click **Save dashboard**, confirm the title, and click **Save**.

The transformation is applied automatically. If it doesn't refresh, click **Refresh** on the panel to verify the visualization looks as intended.

In the next milestone, you'll clean up your columns with an organize fields by name transformation.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [Join by field](/docs/grafana/latest/panels-visualizations/query-transform-data/transform-data/#join-by-field)
